subtitle: Hip abductor — the most undertrained running muscle

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Glute med weakness is the root cause of IT band, knee and lower back issues

mild.description: Outer hip ache during or after runs, Trendelenburg gait on video
mild.can_run_note: Yes — add glute med activation before every run
why: Glute medius weakness allowing the hip to drop on the swing leg side
Prolonged sitting switching off the abductors
Mileage increase outpacing the strength base
relief: Lateral band walks 3 × 15 steps each direction
Hip hitch exercise standing on a step
Ice outer hip after runs if tender
prevention: Clamshells, lateral walks and single-leg squats 3× per week
Record yourself running from behind — check for hip drop

moderate.description: Lateral hip pain during runs, hip drops visibly on video
moderate.can_run_note: Short runs only — prioritise strength work this week
why: Gluteal tendinopathy — compressive load on the tendon insertion at the greater trochanter
Hip abductor strength insufficient for current mileage
Cross-legged sitting and hip adduction compressing the tendon
relief: Avoid crossing legs and deep hip stretches — these compress the tendon
Isometric side-lying hip abduction holds 45s × 5 for pain relief
Sleep with a pillow between knees
prevention: Heavy slow resistance hip abduction — build load progressively
Avoid adducted postures — sitting cross-legged, standing with weight on one hip
Single-leg squat programme for long-term resilience

severe.description: Constant lateral hip pain, pain lying on the hip, cannot run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — gluteal tendinopathy needs load management
why: Significant gluteal tendinopathy requiring structured rehabilitation
Possible trochanteric bursitis needing anti-inflammatory treatment
Chronic tendon degeneration from sustained overload
relief: Physiotherapy assessment for tendon loading programme
Avoid all hip adduction and stretch positions
Corticosteroid injection for bursitis if confirmed
prevention: 8–12 week heavy slow resistance programme before return to running

see_physio_if: Pain lying on the affected side at night
Pain at rest that does not ease
No improvement after 2 weeks of strengthening
